_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: DM (Medical oncology) or equivalent (MD Medicine with experience/Fellowship in Hematology/Medical Oncology)
clinical_indications: Any Myeloproliferative Neoplasm as charted (as per WHO 2016 classification)

clinical_thresholds.note: Triggers IT pop-up/alert when package is booked
Triggers IT pop-up/alert when package is booked

preauth.label: Admission/OPD clinical notes with complete history, physical examinations, indications and plan of treatment

CBC, Peripheral blood smear, Bone marrow aspiration and Biopsy, Cytogenetics and molecular reports (Polycythemia Vera, Chronic myeloproliferative disorders, Myelofibrosis, Essential Thrombocythemia) suggestive of diagnosis of MPN

claim.label: Reports of the tests (pathology, radiology, microbiology, hematology, biochemistry, etc.)
Discharge summary of inpatient department (if admitted for supportive care)
Discharge summary of day care department

ppd_preauth.q: Is a Myeloproliferative Neoplasm diagnosed according to WHO 2016 classification?
Is there a prior hypersensitivity reaction to Hydroxyurea?

cpd_claim.q: Are barcodes of the administered drugs provided?
Are all relevant test reports (pathology, radiology, etc.) attached?
Is an appropriate discharge summary (inpatient or daycare) provided?
Are transfusion slips provided if transfusions were given?
Are OPD visit documents relevant to the claim provided?
Is documentation of follow-up care (CBC, LFT, RFT every 3-4 months) available?

common_queries: Missing or incomplete Admission/OPD clinical notes and treatment plan for pre-authorization.
Missing or incomplete investigation reports for Myeloproliferative Neoplasm diagnosis at pre-authorization stage.
Pre-authorization for Hydroxyurea was approved despite a documented prior hypersensitivity reaction.
Package booked when TLC was not <2500/cumm or platelets were not <50,000/cumm, contrary to IT alert conditions.
Missing barcodes of administered drugs at the time of claim submission.
Missing relevant test reports (pathology, radiology, hematology, biochemistry, etc.) at claim submission.
Discharge summary (inpatient or daycare) or transfusion slips are missing at claim submission.
Follow-up care documentation (monitoring visits with CBC, LFT, RFT every 3-4 months) is incomplete or absent.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Essential Medical Personnel: Qualified Medical Oncologist/Hematologist; Essential Medical Infrastructure: Day care
clinical_key_pointers: Dosage: 500 mg cap, maximum 4 gms per day (depends on WBC count)
Duration: Maximum number of cycles - 12, Minimum duration between 2 cycles 90 days
This package can be given multiple times as and when required
It can be 1st or 2nd line regimen
Supportive care may include Packed RBC and SDP/RDP transfusions.
additional_information.post_op_care: Monitoring visits every 3-4 months with CBC, LFT, RFT
additional_information.referral_criteria: Decision to refer cases to the appropriate level of care as required for treatment of patients under PMJAY.
